name: ConnectorX
pricing_notes: Fully open source (MIT); no API costs

auth.notes: Database credentials in connection string; no service auth

performance.notes: 5-20x faster than pandas read_sql for large queries; Rust-native parallelism; zero-copy to Arrow/Pandas

agent_readiness.notes: Rust-native database connector for loading query results into pandas/polars/Arrow/Dask. Parallel partitioned reads for large datasets. Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite, BigQuery, Redshift, Snowflake, SQL Server, Oracle. 5-20x faster than pandas read_sql. Simple API: cx.read_sql(conn, query, return_type='pandas'). Minimal dependencies.

gotchas: Read-only — only for loading data, not writing
Some SQL dialects have limited support or require workarounds
Partitioned parallel reads require specifying partition column
best_when: Loading large query results from databases into pandas/polars/Arrow fast
Data science workflows where pandas read_sql is a bottleneck
ETL pipelines extracting large datasets from OLAP databases (Snowflake, BigQuery)
ML feature pipelines requiring fast DB-to-DataFrame loading
avoid_when: Small datasets where overhead exceeds benefit
summary: ConnectorX is a Rust-native database connector for loading query results 5-20x faster than pandas read_sql. Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift, SQLite. Outputs to pandas, polars, Arrow, Dask via zero-copy. Simple Python API. Best for data science workflows bottlenecked on large database query loading. Read-only — use SQLAlchemy for write operations.
